2606.11990.title: Time-Series Foundation Model Embeddings for Remaining Useful Life Estimation
2606.11990.paper_summary: Remaining Useful Life (RUL) prediction is essential for industrial predictive maintenance, yet many learning-based approaches rely on extensive feature engineering or large labeled datasets to train task-specific sequence models. In this work, we introduce a lightweight learning approach, in which we leverage a frozen pretrained time-series foundation model (TSFM) and combine it with a small regression head for RUL estimation from multivariate sensor streams. More specifically, we use Chronos-2 as a frozen backbone to extract context window features and train a lightweight regression neural network for RUL prediction. Experiments on real-world industrial sensor data from two device types show that Chronos-2 features consistently improve over recurrent, convolutional, Transformer-based, and gradient-boosting baselines under the same preprocessing and evaluation protocol. We further analyze the impact of context length and find that performance improves significantly with longer histories, indicating that TSFM representation offer a practical and data-efficient alternative for RUL estimation in industrial settings.
2606.11990.paper_summary_zh: 剩餘使用壽命（RUL）預測對於工業預測性維護至關重要，然而許多基於學習的方法依賴於廣泛的特徵工程或大型標記數據集來訓練特定任務的序列模型。<lb/>在這項工作中，我們介紹了一種輕量級的學習方法，其中我們利用一個凍結的預訓練時間序列基礎模型（TSFM），並將其與一個小型回歸頭結合，用於從多變量傳感器流中進行RUL估計。<lb/>更具體地說，我們使用Chronos-2作為凍結的骨幹來提取上下文窗口特徵，並訓練一個輕量級的回歸神經網絡進行RUL預測。<lb/>在來自兩種設備類型的真實工業傳感器數據上的實驗顯示，Chronos-2特徵在相同的預處理和評估協議下，始終優於基於循環、卷積、Transformer和梯度提升的基準。<lb/>我們進一步分析了上下文長度的影響，發現隨著歷史數據的增加，性能顯著提高，這表明TSFM表示為工業環境中的RUL估計提供了一種實用且數據高效的替代方案。
2606.11990.author: Amir El-Ghoussani et.al.
2606.11990.authors: Amir El-Ghoussani, Michele De Vita, Ronald Naumann, Valiseios Belagiannis
